purpose: CORRECTED isolation test (supersedes l9_l10_checkpoint_isolation_test, which used 17.5 RPM/44133566 as its baseline -- that run was discovered to have been overwritten by an abandoned cross-condition warm-start pilot from 22.5 RPM, not a genuine cold-start (t_checkpoint=18.85, omega_b_prev=2.356194 found in its params.json). 30.0 RPM/488db14b verified clean: t_checkpoint=None, omega_b_prev=None, shear_stress.dat starts at t=0.

results.conclusion: On a verified-clean baseline, same-condition checkpoint restart segmenting does NOT meaningfully perturb tau_100_max (-3.0%) or tau_mean_max (-2.6%) -- both small, consistent with ordinary run-to-run/restart numerics. This supersedes the retracted 17.5 RPM result (../l9_l10_checkpoint_isolation_test/), which showed a spurious -17.1% tau_mean_max gap that was actually an artifact of comparing against a corrupted (cross-condition warm-started) baseline, not evidence of real restart contamination. Checkpointing is ruled out as the explanation for the L9-vs-L10 tau_100_max sign flip. See docs_site/explanation/checkpoint-restart.md and docs_site/explanation/kim-et-al-validation.md.
